releases.name: v2.5: DCNet, new on-screen gamepad, DreamConn+ and DreamPicoPort adapters
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* DCNet: a free dedicated VPN service for Dreamcast games that only needs a basic Internet connection. 30 game servers are already available and more are coming.
* Improved on-screen gamepad for Android and iOS: move and resize controls, custom designs, arcade-specific layout, unused controls hidden based on game (arcade) or device (console games).
* Support for physical Dreamcast controllers with DreamConn+ and DreamPicoPort adapters: gamepad, VMU, vibration pack and more.
* New Dreamcast "Full Controller" with 2 analog axes and all possible buttons (Panther DC controller).
* Better analog axis emulation when using a keyboard or digital buttons by using a ramp up/down curve.
* Hardware CD/DVD/BD-ROM support
* GD-ROM arcade games load time reduced to zero.
* Improved GD-ROM access time, particularly with HLE BIOS.
* Fixed: Vulkan per-pixel mode on Mali GPUs.
* Work-around an OpenGLES bug in many drivers displaying the game screen upside down.
* Per-game VMU now compatible with multidisk games.
* Support for some encrypted cheats.
* Online support for Hundred Swords, PBA Bowling, StarLancer and The Next Tetris.

## Fixed Games
* D+VINE
* Doom 64
* Fur Fighters
* King of Fighters XI
* Le Mans 24 Hours
* Manic Panic Ghosts!
* Pro Pinball
* Sega Clay Challenge
* Silent Scope

* World Series Baseball 2K1
* World Series Baseball 2K2

releases.name: U24.07.02-2407
releases.body:
Notable changes from U24.03.22-21259

- Code refactoring and minor fixes
- Fixed a panic alert for Def Jam: Fight for New York and Fifa Street
- Numerous small optimizations to JitARM64
- Small optimizations to DSP HLE audio decoding
- Fixed stuttering from JIT Cache clear - mostly notable in Shadow the Hedgehog
- Set OSD message color to yellow in default AMOLED theme
- Updated Vulkan Memory Allocator and Vulkan Headers
- Updated and optimized app icons

Missing features - these features from Dolphin Official are not implemented

- Android Input Overhaul and subsequent changes to Input and TAS handling
- Custom GPU driver support
- Skylanders portal and Infinity base support
- Gamma correction

This codebase is based on the Official Dolphin dev build 2407-1 (Jul 2, 2024). For more information about what has changed check the PR history. 

**General information**
- When I started this project, my only goal was to add the VBI Skip hack to MMJR2. At the time, I had little to no coding experience and knew nothing about Git, GitHub, or Android Studio. Looking back to 2023, I never imagined I’d come this far.
- Fast forward almost a year and a half later—with over 1,100 pull requests—I’ve managed to update this codebase from Dolphin 5.0-18205 (December 2022) all the way to Dolphin 2407 (July 2024). Of course, all the credit goes to the Dolphin Team, since they did the real heavy lifting. My job has simply been figuring out how to make it all work in MMJR2.
- I work on this fork in my free time, mostly for myself, but I’m genuinely happy that so many of you find it useful. The previous release has been downloaded over 150,000 times and that is honestly mind-blowing. 
- I’ll keep working to improve MMJR2 for as long as I’m able. Thank you all for your support! 
- **Android developers willing to help are welcome!**

**Caution**
- **Save states from older versions are, unfortunately, NOT compatible with this release. Be sure to make an in-game save before updating!**

**Known Issues**
- The UI in the Cheats section is broken when you try to delete multiple items in a row. This bug is present to this day in Dolphin Official. Just delete a few items, exit the cheats window, and return back.
- For people using touch controls. It is possible that the core controls and touchscreen controls get out of sync. For more information check here - [https://github.com/Medard22/Dolphin-MMJR2-VBI/discussions/708](https://github.com/Medard22/Dolphin-MMJR2-VBI/discussions/708)

Missing features - these features from Dolphin Official are not implemented

releases.name: v2.3.000
releases.body:
### **Changelog for v2.3.000 (from v2.2.110)**

- Extensive code refactors including a major overhaul of the graphics pipeline
- Graphics pipeline code revision - removed obsolete MMJR2-specific hacks, that are no longer needed
- Revised and improved Asset Loader
- Updated external components like SDL, Cubeb, mGBA, fmt, etc.
- Updated NDK to v25.2.9519653
- Updated Android libraries, now targeting Java 17
- Many JIT optimizations
- Minor updates to the Vulkan backend
- Optimized Vertex Loader
- Updated GameINI database
- Fixed Fast Forward hotkey
- Added support for WiiConnect24 via WiiLink
- Added support for Color Space Correction
- Leveraged LZ4 algorithm for faster savestate decompression
- Updated and fixed multiple instances of partially synced code across the project

### **Missing features - these features from Dolphin Official are not implemented**

- Android Input Overhaul and subsequent changes to Input and TAS handling
- Custom GPU driver support
- Skylanders portal and Infinity base support
- Gamma correction

**This codebase is roughly equivalent to the Official Dolphin dev build 5.0-20223 (Oct 15, 2023).**

### General information
This release includes over 7 months' worth of code changes ported from Dolphin Official since v2.2.110, and more than 3 months since the last beta. I’ve tested it extensively, but as always, your mileage may vary. 🤷‍♂️ I upgraded my RP3+ to a Retroid Pocket 4 Pro and performed most of the testing on this device.

Android developers willing to help are always welcome! 

### Caution

- Save States from v2.2.110, v2.3.000-rc1, and v2.3.000-rc2 are NOT compatible with this release. Be sure to have in-game saves before updating!
- Due to the graphics pipeline changes, this build bumps the shader cache UID version. Shaders will need to be cached and built from scratch, so expect some initial shader compilation stutter.

### Known Issues

The UI in the Cheats section is broken when you try to delete multiple items in a row. This bug is present to this day in Dolphin Official. Just delete a few items, exit the cheats window, and return back.

As always, have fun and happy gaming ✌️

releases.name: Public beta - v2.3.000-rc2
releases.body:
**Changelog from v2.3.000-rc1**

- Hotfix for Fast Forward Hotkey

This release is identical to v2.3.000-rc1, but I fixed the Fast Forward Hotkey. It was broken for quite a while. Now it toggles between 100% and 200% emulation speed settings.  The real Fast Forward speed will depend on your settings and your device. Slow devices might not reach full  200% speed. 

You can find it in Overlay Controls -> On-screen Hotkey -> Fast Forward

releases.name: Public beta - v2.3.000-rc1
releases.body:
Changelog from v2.2.110

- Many code refactors including a massive overhaul of graphic pipeline
- Graphic pipeline code revision - removed some old and obsolete hacks
- Updated some external components - SDL, Cubeb, mGBA, etc.
- Updated NDK to v25.2.9519653 and some Android libraries
- Minor updates to JIT
- Minor updates to Vulkan backend
- Minor optimization to Vertex Loader
- Updated GameINI database
- Updated out of sync code across the project

This codebase is roughly equivalent to the Official Dolphin dev build 5.0-19479 (Jun 2, 2023).

**General information**
- Hi everyone, It’s been a while since I last worked on the MMJR2 emulator. Unfortunately, work and real-life commitments took a toll on the time I could invest in maintaining MMJR2. I also encountered a significant performance regression that impeded my progress. There are many possible causes for this issue: around June 2023, the Dolphin team added a new asset loader system for graphic mods and custom textures, changed how Dolphin handles threading, and made some updates to the graphics pipeline.
- Since I don’t have as much time anymore, I decided to roll back from Dolphin 5.0-19582 to 5.0-19479 and release a beta version. I made good progress over the months, and it would be a shame not to share it. I did some preliminary testing of this build, and it seems the regressions are not present. However, I didn’t have time to test it thoroughly, hence the beta release.
- I am also waiting for an RP4 Pro and am giving my RP3+ to a friend. While this doesn’t necessarily mean the end of this project, I currently lack a sufficiently low-power device to test new builds on.
- I’ve learned a lot about Git, GitHub, Android development, and Dolphin while working on this project. If you want to help or continue working on MMJR2, let me know. I’d be happy to assist.

**Caution**
- Save States from v2.2.110 are **NOT** compatible with this release. Make sure you have an in-game save before updating.

**Hints**
- For optimal performance on RP3/RP3+ use Performance profile.
- To eliminate shader compilation stuttering enable "Compile Shaders Before Starting". The compiled shaders are usually version/backend specific. Once done you won´t have to compile them again unless you switch to other backend, update the emulator or mess with some settings.

**Known Issues**
- Fast Forward Hotkey is broken. Somehow nobody noticed for a very long time. This is fixed in v2.3.000-rc2.

Changelog for v2.2.110
- Fixed "Compile Shaders Before Starting" hang
- Small fix to FileUtil behavior

I had a report about a performance drop from v2.2.000. After extensive testing, I couldn´t reproduce it on my devices, nor was I able to track it to a specific code change. Every build has a slight minimal variance in performance. This can account for shader compilation, shader cache buildup, SoC thermal throttling, OS state, background apps, etc. When you first run any game on a new version of MMJR2, the shader cache is updated. There might be some minor dips as the shaders are rebuilt and cached that go away after a while, or after you close and reload the game. There might be some performance hits on different SoCs, most likely due to architecture differences and GPU drivers, but I cannot test, nor fix that. If you are affected by a performance hit, you can go back to v2.2.000. Thanks to everyone, who helped me with testing. 

This release codebase is roughly equivalent to the Official Dolphin dev build 5.0-18515.

**Savestates from v2.2.000 and v2.2.100 are compatible.**  You should be able to update without any problems or revert back to older version if you have issues.

releases.name: v.2.2.100
releases.body:
This is a minor update with some handy fixes.

Changelog for v2.2.100
- Improvements to logic op approximation
- Removed obsolete "Skip Approximate Logic Op" hack
- Removed unused force filtering setting
- Fixed Touch IR Pointer mode selection
- Fixed missed custom directory entries - now you can clean game cache files from UI again
- MMJR2 updater is now hidden from the UI - as we all know, it wasn´t working anyway
- Minor code updates and cleanup

This release codebase is roughly equivalent to the Official Dolphin dev build 5.0-18515

**Savestates from v2.2.000 are compatible.** You should be able to update without any problems or revert back to v2.2.000 if you have issues.

releases.name: v.2.2.000
releases.body:
I think it's finally time to stop tinkering and finally release a new version. So here it goes 😄 

**Changelog from v2.1.010 to v2.2.000**

- updated NDK to 25.1.8937393
- updated Android libraries
- Java 11 bytecode is now enabled across project
- code cleanup and refactoring
- minor fixes to JitArm64
- unitialized XFB color changed from pink/magenta/fuchsia to black, making rendering artifacts with "Store XFB copies to Texture only" less noticeable
- exposed "Store XFB Copies to Texture only" speedhack in Quick settings menu
- exposed "Show FPS" in the Quick settings menu
- fixed misaligned OSD messages
- hopefully fixed a throttle bug that made game run at super fast speed after loading state
- removed Autoupdater from the UI, it wasn´t working anyway
- added an option to force linear texture filtering and exposed it in the Android settings
- added sharp bilinear postprocessing shader
- added integer scaling shader
- implemented PPC Data Cache (currently not exposed in Android UI - kills performance)
- implemented CPU Vertex Culling (Graphics Settings->Advanced) - auto-enabled in Metroid Prime and Twilight Princess
- Skylanders Portal support is now in the core (but not exposed in Android due to missing Android UI)
- less confusing version information in the main menu
- other minor fixes and changes

**Known issues**
- When updating from older build, Dolphin may hang on building shader cache.
- If that happens delete shader cache (/mmjr2-vbi/Cache/Shaders/ and .uidcache files in /mmjr2-vbi/Cache/), disable "Compile Shaders Before Starting", run the game, exit emulator and then enable it again.

**Caution**

- **Save States from previous versions of MMJR2-VBI are NOT compatible! Make an in-game save, or you will lose progress!**
- This release will replace any MMJR2 build you might have installed! If the installation fails, uninstall your MMJR2 first. 
- Updating to this release will keep your original MMJR2 data intact. This release uses a separate /mmjr2-vbi folder in the root of your Internal storage.
- This release is incompatible with savestates from other forks (MMJR, MMJR2, Dolphin for Handheld) or Official Dolphin. 
- In-game saves are compatible. You can copy data to respective folders from your Dolphin user directory to the /mmjr2-vbi folder
- I am not an officially registered Android developer. Google Play Protect might get suspicious and give you a warning :)

**Additional information**

- For Daijishō users - this build uses **"106 - gc - Dolphin mmjr"** and **"334 - wii - Dolphin mmjr"** player settings
- All code ported over from Dolphin Official has links to original pull requests and respective authors. I deeply respect their work.
- This is a personal project. Additional updates and releases are possible but not guaranteed. If you enjoy it, great! Otherwise get Official Dolphin from PlayStore.

This release codebase is roughly equivalent to the Official Dolphin dev build 5.0-18439, Jan 30 2023. Don´t expect any massive performance gains, but there are some nice quality-of-life changes. 

Any Android developer willing to help me is welcome. Hell any developer willing to help is welcome 🙈  
The MMJR2 code and especially it´s Android part could use some love. Dolphin went through several massive code overhauls in 2023 and I am not sure how much they break MMJR2. I will try to keep up as long as my limited abilites let me. 

Feedback is welcome in the Discussions. You can also find me on Retroid Discord server. Happy gaming! ✌️

releases.name: v2.1.010
releases.body:
For people coming here from Discord there is an updated version available.

This is a minor update to the initial release. The version identifier of this build is 2.1-18025-r2

- Renamed VI Skip to VBI Skip to match Dolphin Official
- Update UI strings to reflect the custom user directory of this fork
- Fixed Game Covers not stored in the offline cache
- Some fixes to JitArm64 that may fix an uncommon crash I encountered during testing

**Known Issues**
The in-app updater is broken. Please download and sideload the APK manually.

The points mentioned in "Caution" and "Additional Information" in the previous release still stand. Please read them carefully. Happy gaming ✌️

releases.name: v2.1.000
releases.body:
This build of MMJR2 is based on Dolphin 5.0-18025 (Dec 9, 2022) with the following additional features ported over from Official Dolphin. The version identifier of this build is 2.1-18025.

- Improved VPS/FPS counter
- FrameTime/VBlank analyzer + graph
- VBI Skip Hack
- Added VBI Skip hack to the Quick Settings menu

**Caution**
- This release will replace any MMJR2 build you might have installed! If the installation fails, uninstall your MMJR2 first. Make sure you have a backup of the old build in case you want to revert back.
- Updating to this release will keep your original MMJR2 data intact. This release uses a separate /mmjr2-vbi folder in the root of your Internal storage.
- This release is incompatible with savestates from other forks (MMJR, MMJR2, Dolphin for Handheld) or Official Dolphin. 
- In-game saves are compatible. You can copy data to respective folders from your Dolphin user directory to the /mmjr2-vbi folder
- I am not an officially registered Android developer. Google Play Protect might get suspicious and give you a warning :)

**Additional information**
- Performance-wise, it should be comparable to Official Dolphin. Don´t expect any miraculous performance gains.
- For Daijishō users - this build uses **"106 - gc - Dolphin mmjr"** and **"334 - wii - Dolphin mmjr"** player settings
- All code ported over from Dolphin Official has links to original pull requests and respective authors. 
- This is a personal project. Additional updates and releases are possible but not guaranteed. If you enjoy it, great! Otherwise, Official Dolphin is probably a better choice.
- Enjoy :)

**Known issues**
- Game covers are not stored in the local cache. Running the emulator without an internet connection will result in empty covers. This will be fixed in the next release.
- Uncommon crash in LOTR: The Third Age after longer gameplay. According to the log, it´s related to JitArm64. It may affect other games as well. A promising fix is in the testing, it will be part of the next release.

releases.name: XoDos – Rebirth Edition
releases.body:
## 🎊 XoDos  – Rebirth Edition

🌟 Happy Holidays & Happy Birthday XoDos! 🎂

We are thrilled to announce the rebirth of XoDos! As we celebrate the holidays and mark XoDos's birthday, we're proud to share our continued commitment to bringing desktop computing power to Android. This journey continues in our new official repository, where we will keep innovating and expanding XoDos’s capabilities.

---

🔥 XoDos Rebirth – What’s New

The XoDos project has been reborn with renewed energy and vision! We’re continuing development in a fresh repository, building on everything that made XoDos special while pushing toward an even more powerful and integrated future.

🎁 This release marks:

· A fresh start in our new development home
· Continued dedication to the XoDos vision
· The first release in the XoDos rebirth era

---

🚀 XoDos v1.0.3 – Full Release Features

✓  Desktop Environment – Full Linux desktop on Android
✓ Windows Support via Wine  – Run Windows apps 
✓ Multiple Display Options – Termux X11, AVNC, noVNC (remote browser access)
✓ Graphics & Gaming – DXVK, WineD3D, VirGL & Turnip GPU drivers
✓ External Input – Mouse, keyboard, gamepad support (using x11)
✓ Built-in Utilities – Offline tools for developers & power users

---

📦 Download Now

🔗 GitHub Release:,✅
Download XoDos v1.0.3 
https://github.com/xodiosx/XoDos2/releases

🧾 
---

💝 Thank You for Your Support!

To every user, tester, contributor, and supporter – thank you for being part of the XoDos journey. Your feedback and enthusiasm have helped shape this project, and we're excited to continue developing with you in our new repository.

---

📞 Connect With Us

💬 Telegram: t.me/xodemulatorr
🎮 Discord: discord.gg/d2ChVhPfnF
🐙 GitHub: github.com/xodiosx/XoDos2

---

Here's to a new beginning, continued innovation, and bringing desktop freedom to mobile devices. Happy Holidays, Happy Birthday XoDos, and welcome to the Rebirth! 🥳🎉

– The XoDos Team
XoDos by xodios M. aleiw ©2025

releases.name: XoDos v6.0.0
releases.body:
## 🚀 Xodos v6.0 Update - "The All-in-One Powerhouse" 🚀

We're thrilled to announce a massive update for **Xodos**! This release is packed with fixes, optimizations, and incredible new features that transform your experience. Get ready for a smoother, more powerful, and feature-rich Xodos! ✨

### 🐛 What's New & Improved

* 🎮 `FIXED` Gamepad support and bindings for seamless gaming
* 🍷 `FIXED` Wine launching scripts for better compatibility
* 🎨 `FIXED & UPDATED` The user interface for a cleaner look and feel
* 🌙 `FIXED` Desktop dark & light theme bug
* 🧹 `REMOVED` Unnecessary packages and libraries for a leaner app
* 🗑️ `REMOVED` Unused start menu icons and shortcuts to reduce clutter

### 🆕 Brand New Features

#### ⚙️ . Advanced Shortcuts Manager
Create and manage custom shortcuts for each of your Wine games and apps with individual settings!

#### 📁 . Supercharged Files Manager
A powerful file manager with these features:
- ☑️ Multiple Selection Mode
- 📋 Copy & Paste
- 🔒 Change/Fix File Execute Permissions
- 📦 Extract Archives (7z, rar, zip, tar)
- 🔄 Extract/Install/Restore Xodos backups or .tar.xz archives
- ⚡ Run/Open .exe files from anywhere and create shortcuts
- 🎮 Run PlayStation 1 games from anywhere
- 🎯 Run Xbox Original (XEMU) games and manage ISO files
- 🐧 Install .deb Linux packages to native or proot distro

#### 🎮 . Steam Client on Wine (Optional)
Added support for running Steam client inside Wine desktop!
- Installation is optional
- Download Steam from releases and install from start menu

#### 🔄 . Native RetroArch Emulator
Integrated RetroArch directly into desktop with online multiplayer (Netplay) support!

releases.name: XoDos 5.9.0
releases.body:
🚀 **XoDos** Emulator (arm64) - Major Update!

Welcome to a huge update for XoDos! This release packs a massive punch with refinements across the entire app, from a polished UI to powerful new under-the-hood features for both Wine and the Proot environment. Get ready for a smoother, more compatible, and more controllable experience.

✨** What's New in the App?**

· 🎨 Updated & Fixed App UI: A refreshed and more stable user interface for a better overall experience.
· ⚙️ New Quick-Settings Menu: Added new settings for instant access to install DXVK/D3D and drivers for Wine anywhere, anytime! No more complicated commands.
· 🚀 Termux-X11 Performance Boost: Updated to the latest official code! Now featuring hardware buffer support for significantly improved performance and smoothness.
· 📋 Restored Start Menu & Widgets: The convenient start menu is back! Plus, we've added handy Termux widgets for quicker access.
· 🍷 Wine Bionic Updated: Upgraded to Wine 10.0-rc3 x86_64 for much better application and game compatibility.
· 🔧 Fixed Desktop Scripts: Fixed scripts for both Wine Glibc and Bionic. You can now properly stop the desktop from the settings with the "Stop Desktop" button.
· 🖥️ Fixed Native Desktop Launcher: Resolved a bug in the hardware acceleration mode selector that was incorrectly choosing VIRGL mode for Linux apps.
· 🐛 Fixed Proot Desktop Script: The script for launching the Proot desktop has been fixed and stabilized.
· 🖱️ Fixed External Mouse Support: Improved the mouse capturing method for a more reliable external mouse experience.

---

🪄 What's New in the Proot System?

The integrated Proot environment has received a massive upgrade, turning it into a powerhouse for Windows applications.

· 🍷 Wine 9.17: Added the latest and greatest Wine version (9.17) for cutting-edge support.
· 🤖 New Box64 Integration: Added brand-new Box64 for vastly improved x86_64 Windows game and application support on ARM64 devices!
· 🎯 Experimental Turnip Drivers: Added new Turnip drivers (Mesa RadV). They can be installed via .bat files for now. Once stable, they will be integrated into the quick-settings menu just like Bionic and Glibc.
· ⚡ DXVK & Wined3D Installation: Added the ability to install DXVK and Wined3D directly from .bat files inside the Proot environment, same as the drivers.
· 📊 MS Office Installation Fixed: MS Office can now be installed and fixed anytime directly from the desktop.
· 🎮 Updated OpenGL/Zink: Updated and fixed OpenGL and Zink implementations for better graphics rendering.
· 🔬 Beta VIRGL Drivers: Added VIRGL drivers for both Wine and Linux applications (marked as beta for now).
· ...and more! Various under-the-hood tweaks and fixes for a rock-solid experience.

---

📥 Download now and experience the new level of emulation on ARM64!

Note: check the checksum after downloading to make sure files are successfully downloaded 👇

### **XoDos Emulator 5.8 Special Beta Gaming Edition** 🎮✨

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-----------|-------|
| amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-bd2d344040-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-bd2d344040-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |
| Legacy amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-bd2d344040-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-bd2d344040-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness) |
| Steam Deck | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-bd2d344040-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-bd2d344040-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 2 |
| Zen 4 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-bd2d344040-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-bd2d344040-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 4 (AMD Z1/Z2, ROG Ally X, Legion Go S) |
| ARM (aarch64) | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-bd2d344040-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-bd2d344040-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Linux-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-------------------|-------|
| amd64/x86_64 (MSVC) | [MSVC zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Windows-bd2d344040-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | Use if you encounter graphical issues on other builds (e.g. Pokemon Scarlet & Violet) |
| amd64/x86_64 v3 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Windows-bd2d344040-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Windows-bd2d344040-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | Built with MinGW. Requires Ryzen, 4th gen Intel, or newer |
| Zen 4 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Windows-bd2d344040-rog-ally-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Windows-bd2d344040-rog-ally-clang-pgo.zip) | Requires Zen 4 or newer (e.g. ROG Ally X, Legion Go S). Incompatible with Intel |
| aarch64/arm64 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Windows-bd2d344040-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Windows-bd2d344040-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | Snapdragon devices |

## Android

| Build  | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Android-bd2d344040-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [Genshin Spoof APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Android-bd2d344040-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |
| [Legacy APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-Android-bd2d344040-legacy.apk) | For Snapdragon 865 and other unsupported chipsets |

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- [macOS DMG](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-macOS-bd2d344040.dmg)

## Torrent

A torrent containing all artifacts. To use this, simply download the torrent and import it into your
favorite torrent client (BitTorrent only!), and artifacts will be downloaded automatically.
- [Torrent](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780254271.bd2d344040/Eden-bd2d344040.torrent)

| amd64/x86_64 v3 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780167722.ff7bbaea7d/Eden-Windows-ff7bbaea7d-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780167722.ff7bbaea7d/Eden-Windows-ff7bbaea7d-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | Built with MinGW. Incompatible with Intel |

## Android

| Build  | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780167722.ff7bbaea7d/Eden-Android-ff7bbaea7d-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [Genshin Spoof APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780167722.ff7bbaea7d/Eden-Android-ff7bbaea7d-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |
| [Legacy APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780167722.ff7bbaea7d/Eden-Android-ff7bbaea7d-legacy.apk) | For Snapdragon 865 and other unsupported chipsets |

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- [macOS DMG](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780167722.ff7bbaea7d/Eden-macOS-ff7bbaea7d.dmg)

## Torrent

A torrent containing all artifacts. To use this, simply download the torrent and import it into your
favorite torrent client (BitTorrent only!), and artifacts will be downloaded automatically.
- [Torrent](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1780167722.ff7bbaea7d/Eden-ff7bbaea7d.torrent)

- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-----------|-------|
| amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-8fd495f906-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-8fd495f906-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |
| Legacy amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-8fd495f906-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-8fd495f906-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness) |
| Steam Deck | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-8fd495f906-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-8fd495f906-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 2 |
| Zen 4 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-8fd495f906-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-8fd495f906-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 4 (AMD Z1/Z2, ROG Ally X, Legion Go S) |
| ARM (aarch64) | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-8fd495f906-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-8fd495f906-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Linux-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-------------------|-------|
| amd64/x86_64 (MSVC) | [MSVC zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Windows-8fd495f906-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | Use if you encounter graphical issues on other builds (e.g. Pokemon Scarlet & Violet) |
| amd64/x86_64 v3 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Windows-8fd495f906-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Windows-8fd495f906-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | Built with MinGW. Requires Ryzen, 4th gen Intel, or newer |
| Zen 4 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Windows-8fd495f906-rog-ally-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Windows-8fd495f906-rog-ally-clang-pgo.zip) | Requires Zen 4 or newer (e.g. ROG Ally X, Legion Go S). Incompatible with Intel |
| aarch64/arm64 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Windows-8fd495f906-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Windows-8fd495f906-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | Snapdragon devices |

## Android

| Build  | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Android-8fd495f906-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [Genshin Spoof APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Android-8fd495f906-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |
| [Legacy APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-Android-8fd495f906-legacy.apk) | For Snapdragon 865 and other unsupported chipsets |

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- [macOS DMG](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-macOS-8fd495f906.dmg)

## Torrent

A torrent containing all artifacts. To use this, simply download the torrent and import it into your
favorite torrent client (BitTorrent only!), and artifacts will be downloaded automatically.
- [Torrent](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779912217.8fd495f906/Eden-8fd495f906.torrent)

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-----------|-------|
| amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-0d736d49d6-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-0d736d49d6-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |
| Legacy amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-0d736d49d6-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-0d736d49d6-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness) |
| Steam Deck | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-0d736d49d6-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-0d736d49d6-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 2 |
| Zen 4 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-0d736d49d6-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-0d736d49d6-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 4 (AMD Z1/Z2, ROG Ally X, Legion Go S) |
| ARM (aarch64) | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-0d736d49d6-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-0d736d49d6-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Linux-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-------------------|-------|
| amd64/x86_64 (MSVC) | [MSVC zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Windows-0d736d49d6-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | Use if you encounter graphical issues on other builds (e.g. Pokemon Scarlet & Violet) |
| amd64/x86_64 v3 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Windows-0d736d49d6-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Windows-0d736d49d6-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | Built with MinGW. Requires Ryzen, 4th gen Intel, or newer |
| Zen 4 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Windows-0d736d49d6-rog-ally-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Windows-0d736d49d6-rog-ally-clang-pgo.zip) | Requires Zen 4 or newer (e.g. ROG Ally X, Legion Go S). Incompatible with Intel |
| aarch64/arm64 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Windows-0d736d49d6-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Windows-0d736d49d6-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | Snapdragon devices |

## Android

| Build  | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Android-0d736d49d6-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [Genshin Spoof APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Android-0d736d49d6-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |
| [Legacy APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-Android-0d736d49d6-legacy.apk) | For Snapdragon 865 and other unsupported chipsets |

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- [macOS DMG](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-macOS-0d736d49d6.dmg)

## Torrent

A torrent containing all artifacts. To use this, simply download the torrent and import it into your
favorite torrent client (BitTorrent only!), and artifacts will be downloaded automatically.
- [Torrent](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779649503.0d736d49d6/Eden-0d736d49d6.torrent)

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- [macOS DMG](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779219860.e875a3196b/Eden-macOS-e875a3196b.dmg)

## Torrent

A torrent containing all artifacts. To use this, simply download the torrent and import it into your
favorite torrent client (BitTorrent only!), and artifacts will be downloaded automatically.
- [Torrent](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1779219860.e875a3196b/Eden-e875a3196b.torrent)

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-----------|-------|
| amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-4d49341918-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-4d49341918-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |
| Legacy amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-4d49341918-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-4d49341918-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness) |
| Steam Deck | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-4d49341918-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-4d49341918-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 2, with patches for Game/Desktop mode support |
| ROG Ally X | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-4d49341918-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-4d49341918-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 4, with patches for Game/Desktop mode support |
| ARM (aarch64) | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-4d49341918-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-4d49341918-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Linux-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-------------------|-------|
| amd64/x86_64 (MSVC) | [MSVC zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Windows-4d49341918-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | Use if you encounter graphical issues on other builds (e.g. Pokemon Scarlet & Violet) |
| amd64/x86_64 v3 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Windows-4d49341918-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Windows-4d49341918-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | Built with MinGW. Requires Ryzen, 4th gen Intel, or newer |
| Zen 4 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Windows-4d49341918-rog-ally-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Windows-4d49341918-rog-ally-clang-pgo.zip) | Requires Zen 4 or newer (e.g. ROG Ally X, Legion Go S). Incompatible with Intel |
| aarch64/arm64 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Windows-4d49341918-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Windows-4d49341918-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | Snapdragon devices |

## Android

| Build  | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Android-4d49341918-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [Genshin Spoof APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Android-4d49341918-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |
| [Legacy APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-Android-4d49341918-legacy.apk) | For Snapdragon 865 and other unsupported chipsets |

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- [macOS DMG](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778876561.4d49341918/Eden-macOS-4d49341918.dmg)

## Android

|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1778527728.609756db30/Eden-Android-609756db30-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |

  - PR #[3851](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/pulls/3851) merged on 2026-05-09 by John


# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

- [desktop] Fix 2 mod manager bugs (#3884) [`91058d7383`](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/commit/91058d738389490cfb31e9fb4932717f807f6179)

  - PR #[3877](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/pulls/3877) merged on 2026-04-25 by lizzie


# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

  - PR #[3746](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/pulls/3746) merged on 2026-04-11 by xbzk


# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Legacy amd64 | [GCC](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-ci/nightly/releases/download/v1775932386.2c1eff2e60/Eden-Linux-2c1eff2e60-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-ci/nightly/releases/download/v1775932386.2c1eff2e60/Eden-Linux-2c1eff2e60-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-ci/nightly/releases/download/v1775932386.2c1eff2e60/Eden-Linux-2c1eff2e60-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-ci/nightly/releases/download/v1775932386.2c1eff2e60/Eden-Linux-2c1eff2e60-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness)

## Android

| Build  | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| [Standard](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-ci/nightly/releases/download/v1775932386.2c1eff2e60/Eden-Android-2c1eff2e60-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [ChromeOS](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-ci/nightly/releases/download/v1775932386.2c1eff2e60/Eden-Android-2c1eff2e60-chromeos.apk) | For devices running Chrome/FydeOS, AVD emulators, or certain Intel Atom Android devices. |
| [Genshin Spoof](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-ci/nightly/releases/download/v1775932386.2c1eff2e60/Eden-Android-2c1eff2e60-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

| [macOS (DMG)](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-ci/nightly/releases/download/v1775932386.2c1eff2e60/Eden-macOS-2c1eff2e60.dmg) | For Apple Silicon (M1, M2, etc)|

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

releases.name: Horizon V4 - Not Connected fix
releases.body: After minor changes like Box64, Wine (all have almost the same performance), DXVK and Turnip and me not having time to do anything with this project, it's finally getting a "Not Connected" fix related to the usage of /tmp directory on some devices by the termux-x11 library in the app. Should work on Android 15 with this fix as well, people already reported it working

# Added

• Latest Box64 Is Added In Proot Version

• Added Cube D3D & SphereMap 3D Test 

• Added D3D9 Wrapper By ThirteenAG In Start Menu → Install →  WineD3D → Experimental 

• Added Core Fonts

• Added Additional x86 and X64 Visual C Packages

• Added Xna Framework 

• Added An Option To Backup And Import Registry And Saves Of Container 

• Many Other Things Are Also Added 

# New Game Presets 

• Fixed And Added Back Skyrim Game Preset 

• Added Virtua Tennis 3 & 4 Game Preset 

• Added Silent Hill Home Coming Game Preset 

# Changes

• Wine 9.2 Is Added And Made Default In Glibc 

• Game UX Is Disabled By Default ( This Fixes Virtua Tennis 4 And More ) 

• The Fixes Section Has Been Updated 

• Some Games Presets Are Updated 

• Many Other Things Are Changed And Updated 

# Note 

• I Decided To Only Upload Proot_Antutu And Glibc From Now

• Delete The Old Apk And Install The New One 

• Proot Is The Recommended Version 

• Glibc Apk Is Updated Again On Dec 1 2024 . Pls Update

Changes : 

•  Proot Builds Are Now Based On Winlator 8.0 Finally More Compatibility 

• 2.1 Is As Set As The Default GL VERSION

• Disable Vertex Array BGRA Is Disabled By Default 

• Default Gpu Model In Proot Builds Is Now Set To Nvdia GeForce 9800GT So Oblivion Launcher And More Can Detect And Give Settings 

• Box64 Version 0.2.8 From Winlator 7.1 Is Now The Default Version

Added :

• WineD3D 9.20 Added 

• BOX64_MMAP32 Environment Variable Added For Proot Builds

• More Things Are Added Which I Can't Remember Now Sorry 

Updated : 

• Updated The 3D Test ( Use This Instead Cube D3D Test It Is Removed Now )

• Updated VC Redist ( Improves Stability If Installed )

• Prince Of Persia 2008 And Prince Of Persia The Forgotten Sands Game Presets Are Added 

Removed : 

• Lot Of Unnecessary Things Are Removed 

Note : 

• To Fix Audio Issue On PES Games Just Turn Off Wine Esync In Container Settings 

• Skyrim Preset Is Now Removed From Proot Build Because Winlator 8.0 Doesn't Support To Disable Wow64 ( You Can Still Play Skyrim In WinlatorMali 1.8 And 1.7 )

• Delete The Old Apk And Install New One

• Overall Balanced Performance And Improved Compatibility On Proot Builds .

• Proot Is The Recommended Version 

• Glibc Version is Removed On 15 Nov 2024 Will Be Updated Later

### Included Drivers:

   - Standard Turnip driver built from the latest Mesa main branch.
   - Version: `26.0.0-devel`

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v27
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V27
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-unified (turnip/gen8 branch)

I didn't test this this much. Contact me on FEX (#android-wastelands) or EmuCore discord if there are any issues.

For Minecraft use MojoLauncher from this branch: https://github.com/whitebelyash/MojoLauncher/tree/feat/adrenotools (CI: https://github.com/whitebelyash/MojoLauncher/actions/runs/26694388841).


>[!WARNING]
> Please don't report issues to Mesa, PLEASE
          
Supported GPUs:
* Adreno 8XX Family: 840, 830, 829, 825, 810
* Adreno 7XX Family: any GPU supported by the upstream Turnip + 710/720 from Termux patches (unstable)
* Adreno 6XX Family: any GPU supported by the upstream Turnip

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!  
Adreno 810 is additionally very slow, I'm not sure if you even want to use Turnip there

Notes:
* Disable DXVK_HUD if you see weird black artifacts
* Apparently there are swizzling glitches in Eden & maybe other emulators, try increasing render scale(?)
* Some games may require AMD/NVIDIA GPU to run and refuse to start on Qualcomm or block some features. I've included a patch to spoof as Steam Deck - use `TU_DEBUG=deck_emu`
* GMEM rendering produces glitchy picture on A830 (write page faults), disable it with `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. It's pretty much useless for PC emulation anyway.

Changes:
* Import upstream changes
* Start building Turnip with unmerged/draft timeline sync MR applied. This might be broken/unstable/whatever, but it's a proper sync primitives implementation that boosts (OMG!!) framerate in some cases (e.g. Zink) or straight up required for the thing to work (VulkanMod).

releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V25  
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-tu8. All thanks to @curione23 in MojoLauncher.en for this release.

I didn't test this this much. Contact me on FEX (#android-wastelands) or EmuCore discord if there are any issues.

> Please don't report issues to Mesa, PLEASE
          
Supported GPUs:
* Adreno 8XX Family: 840, 830, 829, 825 (please test this one, it might be broken), 810
* Adreno 7XX Family: any GPU supported by the upstream Turnip + 710/720 from Termux patches
* Adreno 6XX Family: any GPU supported by the upstream Turnip

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!

* GMEM rendering produces glitchy picture on A830 (write page faults), disable it with `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. It's pretty much useless for PC emulation anyway.

What's new:
* Upstream rebase (lots of changes from there AGAIN)
* New autotuner! Yay! Though it's probably not that useful on A8XX, additionally as A830 has borked gmem it might be worse than simple sysmem. Remove `TU_DEBUG=sysmem` in Winlator to test this.

* Expose Vulkan 1.3 on devices w/o multiview feature (i.e. Adreno 610). This probably makes Minecraft working on this GPU.

flushall patch is included. KGSL timeline MR is not merged here due to Draft status.

releases.name: Mesa Turnip v26.1.0-git_3
releases.body:
Mesa Turnip V26.1.0-git_3  
Built from: https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa

>[!WARNING]
> GPUs other than A830/A840 may work unstable!

This is a build of current upstream Mesa with my GPU enablement hacks applied

Changes:
- Import upstream as usual
- Decrease reg_size on 830 to 96 - should fix Cyberpunk 2077 crashes. See the commit info.
- Integrate [unmerged](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa/-/merge_requests/39751) KGSL timeline sync MR - works good enough now. This is a proper replacement for old cringy vibecoded timeline sync emulation code I removed few releases ago. 
- Remove gmem cache offset completely as it breaks on lower-end A8XX GPUs.
- due to that, change cache sizes for A825/A810. GMEM should work on A825 now.
- Integrate zdobersek's patch fixing (workaround'ing) FLUSHALL. There's one build now as flushall is disabled by default and fixed.

releases.name: Mesa Turnip v26.1.0-git
releases.body:
Mesa Turnip V26.1.0-git  
Built from: https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa

>[!WARNING]
> GPUs other than A840 may work unstable!

This is a build of current upstream Mesa with my GPU enablement hacks applied

Changes compared to v23:
* Upstream Mesa
* Now doing new builds by applying my patches up on upstream Mesa-git branch instead of cloning my repo (I have updated it as well)
* Removed vibecoded timeline sync emulation shit and other unneeded hacks (read the commit message before blaming me for using AI AI AI)
* Started doing flushall & noflushall builds - flushall is still enabled for A8XX for various reasons
* Various changes in freedreno/tu from Mesa (still no proper A830 config)
* Disabled thin LTO and LTO in general (apparently causes weird bugs in RADV, might also apply for Turnip)
* Maybe something else...

Didn't test this release on my device, but should work well.  

I've also seen interesting Turnip builds with some "cool" fixes, but neither me interested in looking into patches (in sense of asking for sources or searching the repo) nor the author wants to share `git format-patch` of their fork (FWIW I shared all my patches in specific Telegram group), so I care less.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v20
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V20  
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-tu8

>[!WARNING]
> This release is still Beta, especially for devices other than Adreno 840. PLEASE, don't report any issues to Mesa
> Original merge request is Draft again, so it's not ready

Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 810 (tested, working at least in TestD3D) - might be sysmem-only, didn't test gmem
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem

Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:  
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found). It works fine with drm-shim, so maybe same for real devices?

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!  
Additionally A810 cache size is awfully small, so don't expect magic performance on Turnip.

Files:
* gen8 - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)

Notes:
* Disable DXVK_HUD if you see weird black artifacts
* Apparently there are swizzling glitches in Eden & maybe other emulators, try increasing render scale(?)
* Some games may require AMD/NVIDIA GPU to run and refuse to start on Qualcomm or block some features. I've included a patch to spoof as Steam Deck - use `TU_DEBUG=deck_emu`
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

Changes:
* Import upstream changes
* Add proper initial support for Adreno 810. It's now rendering a cube!  
* Add support for A710/A720. These were full copies of A730 with only different ids in termux mesa patches.

The issue with A810 is its very small gmem cache (576KB). This caused an integer underflow, because nobody thought of A810 and its so-small cache, breaking rendering completely as invalid value was written onto `RB_CCU_CACHE_CNTL` register. I have discovered this with a debug build of Mesa, it was hitting an assert even with drm-shim due to this underflow.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX vgralloc-exp
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan Vgralloc-exp (V14)

Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem
Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:
- Adreno 810 (had blackscreen as I assumed it's duo-slice, now the rendering should work, but I'm not sure)
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found)

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!

Files:
* gen8-hacks - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)
* ~~gen8-yuck - branch with some commits reverted to gain back perf in RDR2. Probably useless at this point~~

Settings:
* Disable DXVK_HUD
* ~~Enable swapchain blit path to workaround corrupted image on Android: `WRAPPER_BLIT=1` Not required on GameHub~~
* ~~Activate sysmem on A830 - everything will hang/crash/etc without: `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. Not required on A840/A825~~
Now disabled automatically
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

Changes:
* Added a workaround to fix u_gralloc skipping setting drm format modifier type - all weird pixel artifacts without WRAPPER_BLIT=1 should be gone now!

More info:
Fallback gralloc implementation in Mesa has this check to set proper drm format modifier for ubwc/nonubwc swapchain images (ubwc is a Qualcomm compression):
```c
   if (hnd->handle->numInts >= 2 && hnd->handle->data[hnd->handle->numFds] == gmsm) {
      /* This UBWC flag was introduced in a5xx. */
      bool ubwc = hnd->handle->data[hnd->handle->numFds + 1] & 0x08000000;
      out->modifier = ubwc ? DRM_FORMAT_MOD_QCOM_COMPRESSED : DRM_FORMAT_MOD_LINEAR;
   }
```
Looks like a8xx changed something here thus this condition fails and drm format isn't being set at all. This was the reason of garbled output in most native Vulkan apps.  
Removing the check is enough to overcome this issue.
